section.heading: Consideration of expansion of nuclear forces of other countries

subsection.heading: Report and Certification.—
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 60 days before the President recommends any reductions to the nuclear forces of the United States—

paragraph.chapeau: the President shall transmit to the appropriate congressional committees a report detailing, for each country with nuclear weapons, the high-, medium-, and low- confidence assessment of the intelligence community (as defined in section 3(4) of the National Security Act of 1947 (50 U.S.C. 3003(4))) with respect to—

subparagraph.content: the number of each type of nuclear weapons possessed by such country;
the modernization plans for such weapons of such country;
the production capacity of nuclear warheads and strategic delivery systems (as defined in section 495(e)(2) of this title) of such country;
the nuclear doctrine of such country; and
the impact of such recommended reductions on the deterrence and extended deterrence capabilities of the United States; and

paragraph.chapeau: the Commander of the United States Strategic Command shall certify to the appropriate congressional committees whether such recommended reductions in the nuclear forces of the United States will—

subparagraph.chapeau: impair the ability of the United States to address—

clause.content: unplanned strategic or geopolitical events; or

subparagraph.content: degrade the deterrence or assurance provided by the United States to friends and allies of the United States.

subsection.heading: Form.—
subsection.content: The reports required by subsection (a)(1) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.

subsection.heading: Appropriate Congressional Committees Defined.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “appropriate congressional committees” means the following:

paragraph.content: The Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 112–239, div. A, title X, § 1036(a), Jan. 2, 2013, 126 Stat. 1924; amended Pub. L. 113–291, div. A, title X, § 1071(c)(10), Dec. 19, 2014, 128 Stat. 3509.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2014—Subsec. (a)(1). Pub. L. 113–291 substituted “(50 U.S.C. 3003(4))” for “(50 U.S.C. 401a(4))” in introductory provisions.
